pack_purpose: Deterministic narrow follow-up pack for targeted escalation. It defines the crux questions and operational budgets, while source choice may remain model-driven within runtime-enforced tool guardrails.
verification_questions: Does the latest available information materially change the decision, given: BTC short-horizon volatility into the April 16 12:00 ET Binance settlement minute?
How decision-relevant is the remaining contract/source-of-truth ambiguity: Rules name Binance chart UI while verification used Binance API as a proxy for the same market data family?
Are the current blockers still material to final judgment: Exact-minute path risk remains live despite current cushion?
Is the main verification gap still decision-material: The main unverified risk is a normal crypto drawdown or Binance-specific anomaly into the exact settlement minute.?

structured_handoff_focus.contract_ambiguity_reason: Rules name Binance chart UI while verification used Binance API as a proxy for the same market data family
structured_handoff_focus.verification_gap_summary: The main unverified risk is a normal crypto drawdown or Binance-specific anomaly into the exact settlement minute.
structured_handoff_focus.best_countercase_summary: A routine 3-4% downside move or Binance-specific dislocation before noon ET could still flip this exact-minute contract to No.
structured_handoff_focus.main_reason_for_disagreement: Personas mainly differ on how aggressively to discount exact-minute path risk versus the current 72k buffer.
structured_handoff_focus.resolution_mechanics_summary: Yes resolves only if Binance BTC/USDT's 12:00 ET April 16 one-minute candle final Close is strictly greater than 72000.
structured_handoff_focus.freshness_driver: BTC short-horizon volatility into the April 16 12:00 ET Binance settlement minute
decision_blockers: Exact-minute path risk remains live despite current cushion
Verification is still mostly from the same Binance data family rather than an independent settlement-surface capture
The market edge versus Polymarket is modest after synthesis

supporting_persona_summaries.main_thesis: The market's ~90% Yes price is broadly efficient because BTCUSDT is already around 74.7k on Binance with limited time remaining, though single-minute and single-venue settlement risk justify a slightly lower estimate.
main_logical_chain: Start from the market prior of about 90% because this is a straightforward, active crypto threshold market.
Verify the exact contract mechanics and governing source of truth on the Polymarket rules page.
Check Binance documentation and live BTCUSDT data to confirm the settlement surface family and current distance above the threshold.
Conclude that Yes is still the base case, but trim somewhat below market because single-minute and single-venue settlement risk are real.
strongest_supports: Live Binance BTCUSDT checks during the run were around 74.7k, about 3.8% above the threshold.
Only about 43 hours remained until the relevant settlement minute.
strongest_disconfirmers: BTC can move 3-4% in under two days, so the cushion is meaningful but not decisive.
The contract settles on a single Binance one-minute close, creating timing and venue fragility.
unresolved_ambiguities: Polymarket names the Binance website chart as the settlement surface, while this run used API checks as verification rather than literal settlement output.
No dedicated volatility model was run in this pass, so the estimate remains judgment-based rather than distribution-fitted.
supporting_persona_summaries.what_would_change_view: A drop back toward 72k before settlement, or evidence of Binance-specific operational/chart anomalies, would materially reduce confidence in Yes.
supporting_persona_summaries.timing_relevance: Resolution depends on the Apr 16, 2026 12:00 ET Binance BTC/USDT one-minute candle close, corresponding to 16:00 UTC during EDT.
supporting_persona_summaries.source_quality_view: High-quality contract text plus direct Binance verification made the evidence floor adequate; independence is medium because the verification source is the same data family named by the contract.
